commentaries.author: Cyril of Alexandria
commentaries.content: We are all one body and members one of another. Christ obliges us to be united with one another in the bonds of charity. .

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Receive one another, in the spirit of charity, peace, patience, as Christ also hath received you, and bore with your infirmities. (Witham)
Mutually support each other for the glory of God: learn to practise a grand lesson of Christian morality, to bear and to forbear. (Haydock)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Therefore receive one another, as Christ also received us to the glory of God. Let us obey this command and bind ourselves closely to one another. For it is no longer just the weak that he is encouraging, but everyone. If someone wants to break relations with you, do not do the same with him…. Rather, display even more love toward him, that you may draw him to you. For he is a member of the body, and when a member is cut off we must do everything we can to unite it again and then pay more attention to it.
